mcqs.question: What is the primary advantage of dynamic arrays over static arrays?

options.text: Fixed memory allocation at compile time
Ability to grow and shrink during runtime

mcqs.explanation: Dynamic arrays can resize during program execution, providing flexible memory management unlike static arrays with fixed compile-time sizes.

mcqs.question: What is the amortized time complexity of inserting an element at the end of a dynamic array?
mcqs.explanation: While occasional resizing takes O(n), the average (amortized) cost per insertion is O(1) because the resizing cost is distributed across all insertions.

mcqs.question: When does a dynamic array typically trigger a resize operation?

options.text: When array is completely full always

mcqs.explanation: Resizing occurs when the load factor (elements/capacity) exceeds the threshold, typically 0.75, to balance memory usage with performance.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of accessing an element by index in a dynamic array?
mcqs.explanation: Dynamic arrays provide O(1) random access because elements are stored contiguously in memory, allowing direct index calculation.

mcqs.question: What is the typical growth factor used when resizing dynamic arrays?
mcqs.explanation: Most implementations use a growth factor of 2 (doubling), which provides amortized O(1) insertions by making total resizing cost O(n).

mcqs.question: Which operation has O(n) time complexity in dynamic arrays?
mcqs.explanation: Insertion at the beginning requires shifting all elements (O(n)), and linear search in unsorted array also takes O(n) time.

mcqs.question: What happens when elements are deleted from a dynamic array?

options.text: Elements are shifted to fill the gap; array may shrink if load factor is too low
A hole is created that remains unfilled

mcqs.explanation: Deleted elements create gaps that must be filled by shifting subsequent elements. The array may shrink when load factor drops below threshold (e.g., 0.25).

mcqs.question: What is 'load factor' in dynamic arrays?

options.text: Maximum number of elements the array can hold
Ratio of current elements to capacity
The factor by which array grows

mcqs.explanation: Load factor = number of elements / capacity. It determines when to resize: typically grow when > 0.75, shrink when < 0.25.

mcqs.question: Which data structure is better for frequent insertions at the beginning of the sequence?
mcqs.explanation: Linked lists provide O(1) insertion at any position, while dynamic arrays require O(n) to shift all elements when inserting at the beginning.

mcqs.question: What is 'shrink-to-fit' in dynamic array implementation?

options.text: Reducing array size to zero after every deletion
Reducing capacity when load factor drops below threshold to save memory
Compressing the array to remove gaps

mcqs.explanation: Shrink-to-fit reduces allocated memory when load factor becomes too low (e.g., < 0.25), preventing memory waste.

mcqs.question: What is the space complexity of a dynamic array that currently holds n elements?
mcqs.explanation: Space complexity is O(n) as the array stores n elements, though capacity may be up to 2n (when half full after doubling).

mcqs.question: What is a disadvantage of dynamic arrays compared to linked lists?

options.text: Expensive insertion and deletion in the middle

mcqs.explanation: Dynamic arrays require O(n) time to insert/delete in the middle due to shifting elements, while linked lists do this in O(1).

mcqs.question: In C++, which STL container implements a dynamic array?
mcqs.explanation: std::vector in C++ STL implements a dynamic array with automatic resizing and O(1) amortized push_back operations.

mcqs.question: Why is contiguous memory allocation beneficial for dynamic arrays?

options.text: Enables efficient CPU cache utilization and fast access
Allows storage of any data type

mcqs.explanation: Contiguous memory allows the CPU to load data in cache lines, significantly improving access speed through spatial locality.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of searching for an element in a sorted dynamic array?
mcqs.explanation: Binary search on a sorted array with random access takes O(log n) time, utilizing the O(1) access feature.

mcqs.question: When a dynamic array doubles its capacity, what is the time complexity of the resizing operation?
mcqs.explanation: Resizing requires allocating new memory and copying all n elements to the new array, taking O(n) time.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a typical threshold for dynamic array resizing?
mcqs.explanation: A threshold of 1.0 would mean waiting until array is completely full, which would cause resizing on every subsequent insertion, leading to O(n) per insertion.

mcqs.question: What is the main reason for using a growth factor less than 2 (e.g., 1.5) in some implementations?
mcqs.explanation: A 1.5 growth factor wastes less memory compared to doubling (2), as the capacity doesn't exceed the actual needed space by a large factor.

mcqs.question: In Python, which data structure is implemented as a dynamic array?
mcqs.explanation: Python's list is implemented as a dynamic array that automatically resizes, allowing append, insert, and remove operations.

mcqs.question: What happens to iterator validity when a dynamic array is resized?
mcqs.explanation: When a dynamic array resizes, all iterators pointing to elements become invalid because elements are moved to a new memory location.
